channel.title: IT-Security & Privacy News Aggregator
channel.description: Aggregierter RSS-Feed aus führenden IT-Security, Privacy und Cyber Policy Quellen

item.title: Best Vulnerability Management Tools & Software in 2026 | UpGuard
item.description: Discover the best vulnerability management tools on the market and learn which vulnerability detection solution is best for your organization.

item.title: Best Cyber Threat Detection Tools & Software | UpGuard
item.description: The best cyber threat detection tools and software in 2026. Learn how to protect your endpoints, logs, and external digital surfaces from a breach.

item.title: Best Data Breach & Data Leak Detection Tools | UpGuard
item.description: Stopping an intrusion is half the battle. This guide ranks the top breach and leak detection tools to neutralize exposed credentials before attackers do.

item.title: Best Threat Intelligence Platforms and Vendors | UpGuard
item.description: Compare the best threat intelligence platforms (TIPs) and vendors of 2026. Learn key features, criteria, and find the perfect tool for your security team.

item.title: Fresh SharePoint Vulnerability Exploited Soon After Disclosure
item.description: <p>The critical-severity security defect allows remote, authenticated attackers to execute arbitrary code on the server.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/fresh-sharepoint-vulnerability-exploited-soon-after-disclosure/">Fresh SharePoint Vulnerability Exploited Soon After Disclosure</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: CISA Adds Exploited SharePoint RCE Zero-Day CVE-2026-58644 to KEV
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Thursday added a newly patched security flaw impacting Microsoft SharePoint Server to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, requiring Federal Civilian Executive Branch (FCEB) agencies to apply the fixes by July 19, 2026.
The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-58644 (CVSS score: 9.8), a critical deserialization
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Coca-Cola Suspends US Fairlife Production Due to Ransomware Attack
item.description: <p>The company says the incident has not affected product quality and safety, nor Fairlife’s Canada production.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/coca-cola-suspends-us-fairlife-production-due-to-ransomware-attack/">Coca-Cola Suspends US Fairlife Production Due to Ransomware Attack</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Agentic AI Is Untamable: Ask the Right Security Questions
item.description: Forget about attackers. Agentic artificial intelligence is creating enough risks for organizations and demands a security reframe.
item.dc:creator: Arielle Waldman

item.title: 1M+ Emails Use Hidden Text to Dupe AI Security Filters
item.description: Artificial intelligence and LLMs can be surprisingly ineffective against text salting, allowing phishing emails to slide right into your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: The Biggest Data Breaches of 2026 So Far, Ranked by Impact
item.description: <p>The biggest data breaches of 2026 so far, ranked by impact, with details on exposed data, affected users, and what readers should do next.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-biggest-data-breaches-2026-ranked-impact/">The Biggest Data Breaches of 2026 So Far, Ranked by Impact</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Matt Gonzales

item.title: Two Scattered Spider Hackers Get 5.5 Years Each for £29 Million TfL Hack
item.description:
Owen Flowers, 18, and Thalha Jubair, 20, were each sentenced to five and a half years at Woolwich Crown Court on Thursday, 16 July 2026, for the 2024 hack of Transport for London.
The attack left 148 TfL systems inoperable and forced all 27,000 of the transport authority's employees into an office to get their passwords reset in person. Both the NCA and the CPS put TfL's losses and recovery
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Google’s Android App Store Shake-Up Starts July 22
item.description: <p>Google Play will open catalog access to third-party US Android app stores on July 22, following a court order tied to Epic Games’ antitrust case.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-google-play-third-party-android-app-stores/">Google’s Android App Store Shake-Up Starts July 22</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: Best Dark Web Monitoring Tools, Software, and Services in 2026 | UpGuard
item.description: Choose the right tool to manage your exposure with our 2026 breakdown of the best dark web monitoring tools and software for your organization.

item.title: ThreatsDay: Game Cheat Spyware, 24-Hour Ransomware, Chrome Sync Stalking + 12 More Stories
item.description:
A lot of this week’s trouble starts with something that looks close enough.
A familiar repo. A useful installer. A harmless sync setting. Then the handoff goes bad, the box starts talking to someone else, and the damage moves faster than the explanation.
Old bugs are back, weak defaults are earning their keep, and some attack paths are so plain they barely feel like research. Here’s the mess.
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Legacy Systems, Real-World Impacts: The Reality of OT Security
item.description: <p>Legacy systems, safety concerns, and critical infrastructure risks make OT vulnerability disclosure one of cybersecurity's most challenging balancing acts.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/legacy-systems-real-world-impacts-the-reality-of-ot-security/">Legacy Systems, Real-World Impacts: The Reality of OT Security</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Tod Beardsley

item.title: Phishing Campaign Hides Lua Loader as TrueType Font File
item.description: Global phishing campaign disguised a Lua loader as a font file to deploy RATs and infostealers

item.title: Protecting Privacy in an AI Era
item.description: <p>Daniel Solove <a href="https://www.wsj.com/tech/cybersecurity/ai-privacy-laws-data-26d9769f">argues</a> in the <i>Wall Street Journal</i> (alternate <a href="https://archive.is/gEhP5">link</a>) that giving people control of their personal data is not an effective way to regulate privacy in this era. Instead, we need to hold companies accountable for their actions, similar to what we do with food and drug companies. Measures such as rigorous data minimization, fiduciary duties, liability for negligent or reckless technological design, liability for algorithms that cause harm, and multi-stakeholder review of technologies will be far more effective.</p> <p><a href="https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=6985419">Paper</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: n8n Token Exchange Flaw Could Let Attackers Log In as Users From Another Issuer
item.description:
n8n, the workflow automation platform, handed out the wrong accounts at login. On Enterprise instances configured to trust more than one external token issuer, it matched an incoming JWT to a local user on the&nbsp;sub&nbsp;claim alone and ignored&nbsp;iss.
A valid token from issuer A carrying a&nbsp;sub&nbsp;that belongs to someone under issuer B logged you in as them. Their password never
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Modular macOS Stealer Uses Kill Loops to Force Password Entry
item.description: New ClickLock macOS stealer locked victims out of their own system until they surrendered a password

item.title: Single Prompt Enables ChatGPT to Execute Full Cyber-Attack Chain, Researchers Claim
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers tested Open AI GPT 5.5’s offensive cyber capabilities – and the results showed how effective a frontier LLM can be for hackers

item.title: Two Scattered Spider Hackers Sentenced to Jail in UK
item.description: <p>Thalha Jubair and Owen Flowers were prosecuted over a 2024 cyberattack targeting Transport for London (TfL).</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/two-scattered-spider-hackers-sentenced-to-jail-in-uk/">Two Scattered Spider Hackers Sentenced to Jail in UK</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: AI Data Centers Are Being Built Faster Than They Can Be Secured
item.description: <p>AI infrastructure introduces new security risks that traditional data center designs were never built to handle.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/ai-data-centers-are-being-built-faster-than-they-can-be-secured/">AI Data Centers Are Being Built Faster Than They Can Be Secured</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kevin Townsend

item.title: New TELEPUZ Malware Spreads via ClickFix to Steal Data and Run Commands
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have called attention to a new modular malware called TELEPUZ that's been spreading via websites infected with ClickFix lures since late April 2026.
"The malware is full-featured, lightweight, and modular," Elastic Security Labs researcher Cyril François said in a technical report. "While the number of C2 [command-and-control] domains is currently small, the daily
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: ‘ClickLock Stealer’ Bypasses macOS Security With Social Engineering, Process Killing
item.description: <p>The new macOS malware has targeted at least 100 users to steal their passwords and cryptocurrency. </p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/clicklock-stealer-bypasses-macos-security-with-social-engineering-process-killing/">&#8216;ClickLock Stealer&#8217; Bypasses macOS Security With Social Engineering, Process Killing</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: New ClickLock macOS Stealer Kills Apps Every 210ms Until Victims Type Their Password
item.description:
ClickLock Stealer, a new macOS infostealer, answers a victim's refusal by killing their apps on a loop until they hand over the login password. It arrives as a command pasted into Terminal, asks for the password behind a fake system dialog, and when the victim cancels, installs two LaunchAgents and quietly exits.
At the next login, Finder, the Dock, Spotlight, Terminal, Activity Monitor, and
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: 20+ Hijacked Government Websites Became an Attack Channel
item.description:
More than 20 Brazilian government websites were hijacked and turned into malware delivery channels in an active PhantomEnigma campaign uncovered by ANY.RUN, a leading provider of interactive malware analysis and threat intelligence solutions.
The investigation revealed previously undocumented backdoor behavior, hidden infrastructure relationships, and multiple attack arms behind a campaign
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: "Selfish Bravado" Behind TfL Cyber-Attack, Judge Says as Pair Jailed
item.description: The perpetrators of the 2024 TfL cyber-attack have been jailed for five and a half years each after pleading guilty to Computer Misuse Act offences

item.title: New Agent Data Injection Attack Can Make AI Agents Misclick or Run Attacker Commands
item.description:
Ask an AI agent to summarize the reviews on a product page, and a single planted review can make it click "Buy Now" instead. Ask a coding assistant to apply a maintainer's fix from a GitHub thread, and a fake comment can make it run a stranger's command on your computer.
Neither trick hijacks the agent's task. Each one just corrupts the facts it trusts and lets it carry on with the job you
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Oak Emerges From Stealth Mode With $60 Million in Funding
item.description: <p>The startup has built an AI-powered Identity Operating System that governs all identities across an organization’s environment.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/oak-emerges-from-stealth-mode-with-60-million-in-funding/">Oak Emerges From Stealth Mode With $60 Million in Funding</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Daxin Resurfaces in Taiwan Alongside Stupig Pre-Login SYSTEM Backdoor
item.description:
An advanced malware previously attributed to a China-linked threat actor has resurfaced after more than four years within a Taiwan manufacturing firm, along with a previously unreported backdoor dubbed Stupig.
Daxin ("srt64.sys"), as the kernel-mode rootkit is referred to, was first documented by Broadcom-owned Symantec in March 2022, with evidence indicating its use in targeted attacks aimed
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Splunk, Zoom Patch Critical Vulnerabilities
item.description: <p>The flaws could allow attackers to access credentials and data, take over accounts, and escalate their privileges.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/splunk-zoom-patch-critical-vulnerabilities/">Splunk, Zoom Patch Critical Vulnerabilities</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: AI Can Find Bugs, But Human Knowledge Still Proves Them
item.description: Artificial intelligence (AI) is changing offensive security, but it has not changed the standard that matters most: a finding has to be proven before it becomes useful. AI-assisted tools can read code quickly, generate payloads, summarize attack surfaces, explain unfamiliar APIs, and run repetitive testing workflows at impressive speed. That is a real advantage for security teams. It also
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: SANS Warns of AI Governance Gap as Use by Security Teams Surges
item.description: SANS Institute says governance programs are still nascent even as AI failures and threats grow

item.title: Unpatched Shark Vacuum Flaw Could Let Attackers Control Other Vacuums Region-Wide
item.description:
Pull the certificate off the flash of a Shark RV2320EDUS robot vacuum, and you can run root commands on other people's Shark vacuums across the same AWS region: watch the camera, drive the robot, read the map of the house, and take the Wi-Fi password in plaintext.
A researcher publishing under the handle tokay0&nbsp;put the method online&nbsp;on Monday, having tested it only against vacuums he
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: F5 Patches Multiple NGINX, BIG-IP Vulnerabilities
item.description: <p>Attackers could exploit the bugs to modify configurations, terminate or restart processes, cross security boundaries, leak memory, and execute code.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/f5-patches-multiple-nginx-big-ip-vulnerabilities/">F5 Patches Multiple NGINX, BIG-IP Vulnerabilities</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: US Launches Gold Eagle to Coordinate AI-Driven Vulnerability Management
item.description: The White House announced Gold Eagle to help accelerate the discovery, prioritization and patching of flaws found by AI

item.title: China’s Top Cybersecurity Firms Hit by Mounting Military Procurement Bans
item.description: <p>Chinese cybersecurity firms are facing action from the country’s military, but it’s not due to product or technical failures.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/chinas-top-cybersecurity-firms-hit-by-mounting-military-procurement-bans/">China&#8217;s Top Cybersecurity Firms Hit by Mounting Military Procurement Bans</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: OpenAI’s GPT-Red Automates Prompt Injection Testing to Harden GPT-5.6 Sol
item.description:
OpenAI has disclosed details of GPT-Red, an internal automated red-teaming model that scales prompt injection vulnerability discovery with an aim to fix issues before the tools are deployed widely.
"GPT‑Red is a strong red-teamer, and our previous models are highly vulnerable to its prompt injection attacks," the artificial intelligence (AI) company said. "We use GPT‑Red to adversarially train
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Zoom Patches Critical Windows Flaw That Could Enable Account Takeover
item.description:
Zoom has released security updates for a critical security flaw impacting Zoom Workplace for Windows that could facilitate account takeover.
The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-53412 (CVSS score: 9.8), affects Zoom Workplace for Windows before version 7.0.0 and Zoom Workplace VDI Client for Windows before version 7.0.10, 6.6.15, and 6.5.18 in their respective branches.
"Improper Input
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Police Disrupt a €140M Cyber Fraud Ring in Spain
item.description: Iberian hackers carried out a variety of cyberattacks and laundered the winnings through complex financial networks.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: Forgotten Bootloaders Expose Secure Boot Blind Spot
item.description: Nearly a dozen vulnerable and now revoked UEFI shim bootloaders remained trusted for years, giving attackers a path to bypass Secure Boot.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: Identity Attacks Overtake Exploits as Top Ransomware Cause
item.description: Email attacks overtook exploits as the top ransomware root cause last year. Multifactor authentication (MFA) was deployed in 97% of credential-based attacks but failed to prevent compromise.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: TuxBot v3 Evolution Shows Signs of LLM-Assisted IoT Botnet Development
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a previously unreported Internet-of-Things (IoT) botnet framework dubbed TuxBot v3 Evolution that shows signs of being developed with assistance from a large language model (LLM), albeit with not so successful results.
"While the AI complied with their request to generate botnet code, it included a safety disclaimer that the developer failed
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Jalisco, OmegaLord Phishing Kits Target Microsoft 365 Accounts
item.description: <p>New Jalisco and OmegaLord phishing kits target Microsoft 365 accounts by abusing device code flows, OAuth tokens, and MFA prompts to maintain access.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-microsoft-365-phishing-kits-mfa-bypass/">Jalisco, OmegaLord Phishing Kits Target Microsoft 365 Accounts</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: Guten Tag, Bonjour, Hola to Our European Cyber Defenders!
item.description: We're thrilled to unveil the latest evolution of Dark Reading's DR Global section — your go-to source for region-specific cybersecurity intelligence beyond North America.
item.dc:creator: Tara Seals

item.title: Is 'Tech-xit' Imminent? UK Steps Up Sovereignty Push Amid AI Strife
item.description: The US government's restrictions on Anthropic and OpenAI frontier models have intensified calls in the UK and other countries to reduce their reliance on US tech companies, with significant cyber implications.
item.dc:creator: Rob Wright

item.title: Microsoft Fixes Record 570 Security Flaws in Biggest Patch Tuesday Ever
item.description: <p>Microsoft fixed a record 570 security flaws in July 2026 Patch Tuesday, including three zero-days and two exploited bugs.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-microsoft-july-2026-patch-tuesday/">Microsoft Fixes Record 570 Security Flaws in Biggest Patch Tuesday Ever</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: CMMC Assessment Pause Leaves Defense Contractors Facing a New Risk
item.description: <p>The CMMC assessment pause doesn't remove cybersecurity obligations. Here's why defense contractors should treat it as an opportunity to strengthen governance and AI oversight.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-cmmc-pause-ai-governance-defense-contractors/">CMMC Assessment Pause Leaves Defense Contractors Facing a New Risk</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Tim Freestone

item.title: Apple Warns Millions of iPhone Users: FaceTime Scams Are Spreading
item.description: <p>Apple is warning iPhone users about FaceTime scams. Learn how the fraud works, what Apple recommends, and how to report suspicious calls.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-apple-facetime-scam-warning-iphone-users/">Apple Warns Millions of iPhone Users: FaceTime Scams Are Spreading</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Matt Gonzales

item.title: OkoBot Malware Framework Injects Seed Phrase Phishing Into Ledger and Trezor Apps
item.description:
A malware framework called OkoBot has been running on Windows machines since April 2025, and one of its modules is built to con hardware wallet owners out of their recovery phrase.
On an infected PC, the request comes from inside the wallet's own desktop software. Sometimes it waits until you plug the device in first. The page is malicious. The app around it is the real one you installed, and
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Meet the Council’s New Head of Business Operations and Risk Management
item.dc:creator: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Claude Flaw Automatically Sends Malicious Prompts to AI Agents
item.description: When combined with another exploit, the &quot;PromptFiction&quot; vulnerability, which has been fixed, could have enabled an end-to-end attack on a targeted system.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: Phishing Campaign Abuses eCards to Deploy RMM Tools
item.description: Six-month phishing campaign used seasonal eCard lures to plant legitimate RMM tools on victims

item.title: Microsoft Urges Windows 11 Users to Install Updates Within Three Days
item.description: <p>Microsoft now recommends installing Windows 11 quality updates within three days, citing AI-driven cyberattacks that can exploit new vulnerabilities faster than ever.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-microsoft-windows-11-updates-three-day-recommendation/">Microsoft Urges Windows 11 Users to Install Updates Within Three Days</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: Eleven Vulnerable UEFI Shims Enable Secure Boot Bypass
item.description: Eleven forgotten Microsoft-signed UEFI shims can bypass Secure Boot on almost any machine

item.title: Firefox, Chrome, Adobe, and VMware Updates Fix Multiple Critical Security Flaws
item.description:
Mozilla has released updates to address two critical flaws in Firefox for which it warned that exploit code has been published.
The vulnerabilities are listed below -
CVE-2026-15718, an invalid pointer in the JavaScript: WebAssembly component CVE-2026-15719, a site isolation in the DOM: Navigation component
"We are aware that exploit code for this is public, however we are not aware of
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: 2-Click Cursor Exploit Enables Dev Environment Takeover
item.description: Simple age-old bugs give bad actors access to developers' secrets and source code-rich environments.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: Compromised Logins Surge as the Most Common Entry Point for Ransomware Attacks
item.description: Research of incidents by Sophos finds that phishing, brute force attacks and other identity-based threats have surpassed software vulnerabilities as means of delivering ransomware

item.title: Apple Tests New iMessage Warning for Malicious Messages
item.description: <p>Apple is testing an iOS 26.6 security alert that warns users about malicious iMessages and lets them report suspicious messages.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-ios-26-6-malicious-imessage-warning/">Apple Tests New iMessage Warning for Malicious Messages</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kezia Jungco

item.title: Progress Restores ShareFile Storage Zones Access After Security Warning
item.description: Progress has restored access to its ShareFile Storage Zones Controller after a four-day suspension triggered by a credible external security threat

item.title: SASE Has An AI Blind Spot. Inspecting Packets Is No Longer Enough.
item.description:
For years, routing traffic through cloud proxies was good enough. Then work moved to the browser, AI entered the workflow, and the inspection model stopped keeping up.
Enterprise workflows now live across SaaS applications, browsers, and an expanding ecosystem of generative AI tools, unsanctioned browser extensions, and autonomous agents. Employees routinely paste intellectual property into
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Researcher Drops New Windows Zero-Day PoC Hours After Microsoft Patch Tuesday
item.description:
Security researcher Chaotic Eclipse (aka Nightmare-Eclipse) has released a new proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit called LegacyHive.
It has been described as a Windows User Profile Service arbitrary hive load elevation of privileges vulnerability. The Windows User Profile Service, also referred to as ProfSvc, is a core system component that manages user accounts and environments.
"The PoC requires
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: New Webinar: Closing the Approval Gap in AI-Era Ad Tech
item.description:
A single approved marketing tag can quietly load fourth-party code your security team has never seen, granting full access to your forms, customer data, and checkout pages.
This on-demand webinar reveals how this Approval Gap forms, and gives your team the blueprint to close it before an auditor, regulator, or attacker finds it first.
The Reality of the Approval Gap
It's a pattern every
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: A Video Screen That Is Also a Camera
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Cursor Flaw Lets Malicious Cloned Repositories Trigger Windows Code Execution
item.description:
Open a repository in Cursor&nbsp;on Windows and, if a file named&nbsp;git.exe&nbsp;is sitting in the project root, Cursor runs it. No click, no approval dialog, no warning that anything in the folder is about to execute.
Whatever that binary does, it does as you, with your source, your SSH keys and your cloud tokens. Cursor keeps re-running it for as long as the project stays open.
No prompt
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Microsoft Patches 570 CVEs in Record Patch Tuesday
item.description: Microsoft released fixes for a record 570 CVEs in its July Patch Tuesday update, as experts warn AI is dramatically accelerating vulnerability discovery and increasing patch volumes

item.title: Compromised AsyncAPI npm Packages Deliver Multi-Stage Botnet Malware
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Government Updates UK’s National Risk Register with Cyber Warnings
item.description: The UK government is warning of the potential impact of catastrophic cyber-attacks

item.title: Nigeria Deepens Cybersecurity Efforts as Cybercriminals See More Profits
item.description: The West African country advanced rules to force organizations to disclose cyberattacks, joining other nations in a shift to mandated transparency.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: Two SonicWall SMA 1000 Zero-Days Exploited, One Could Enable Admin Commands
item.description:
SonicWall has warned of active exploitation of two zero-day vulnerabilities impacting Secure Mobile Access (SMA) 1000 series appliances, one of which could be exploited to achieve arbitrary command execution.
The vulnerabilities are listed below -
CVE-2026-15409 (CVSS score: 10.0) - A Server-side request forgery (SSRF) vulnerability that a remote unauthenticated attacker could exploit to
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Cribl Adds Agentic Detection Engineering &amp; Boosts SecOps With CardinalOps Deal
item.description: CardinalOps will give Cribl customers the ability to map detection rules and security controls to the MITRE ATT&amp;CK framework. SecOps teams can identify coverage gaps and operationalize threat intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Schwartz

item.title: Records Are Made to Be Broken: Patch Tuesday Raises Triage Stakes
item.description: Three of the 622 CVEs for which Microsoft issued patches this week are zero-days; there are more than 60 critical vulnerabilities.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: Lidl Data Breach Exposes Customer Details in Germany, Belgium, and the Netherlands
item.description: <p>Lidl says attackers stole customer data in Germany, Belgium, and the Netherlands via a third-party provider, raising concerns about phishing and GDPR compliance.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-lidl-data-breach-germany-belgium-netherlands-emea/">Lidl Data Breach Exposes Customer Details in Germany, Belgium, and the Netherlands</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kezia Jungco

item.title: Microsoft Patches Record 622 Flaws, Including Two Zero-Days Under Active Attack
item.description:
Microsoft shipped its largest Patch Tuesday on record today, and two of the fixes close holes that attackers are already exploiting. The release covers 622 of Microsoft's own CVEs by its&nbsp;Security Update Guide&nbsp;count, more than triple&nbsp;June's previous high of around 200.
Those two live bugs are the ones to grab first. Microsoft credits incident responders for both. Both are
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: 6 GHz Wi-Fi Flaws Could Disrupt Critical Systems
item.description: Automated Frequency Coordination systems by default trust client-side data, which could lead to location spoofing and other attacks that disrupt traffic.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Microsoft Patches a Record 570 Security Flaws
item.description: Microsoft Corp. today released software updates to plug at least 570 security holes in its Windows operating systems and other software, almost triple the number of vulnerabilities the software giant fixed in its record-smashing Patch Tuesday release last month. Microsoft attributed the burgeoning patch counts to vulnerability discoveries aided by artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: BrianKrebs

item.title: SAP Patches CVSS 9.9 NetWeaver ABAP Flaw That Could Expose or Modify Data
item.description:
SAP has rolled out updates to address multiple vulnerabilities as part of its July 2026 security updates, including a critical flaw in SAP NetWeaver Application Server ABAP.
The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-44747 (CVSS score: 9.9), an out-of-bounds write flaw that allows an authenticated attacker to leverage logical errors in memory management to cause a memory corruption that could
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Manage Vendor Risk in a Few Practical Steps
item.description: Risk tolerance, exposure visibility, board oversight — handling third-party risk is complicated but achievable with disciplined, precise governance.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Nutkis

item.title: Researchers Say Claude for Chrome Flaw Lets Rogue Extensions Trigger Gmail Reads
item.description:
Any other browser extension that can run a script on claude.ai can still trigger Claude for Chrome tasks aimed at your Gmail, your latest Google Doc and its comments, and your Calendar.
Both this and ClaudeBleed need a rogue extension that can already run a script on claude.ai; the difference is scope. Anthropic restricted the arbitrary-prompt path in May as part of its response to the&nbsp;
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: LabubaRAT Masquerades as NVIDIA Software to Control Windows Hosts
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a previously undocumented Rust-based remote access trojan (RAT) codenamed LabubaRAT that masquerades as NVIDIA software to blend into target environments.
"LabubaRAT creates a reusable foothold for hands-on activity," Blackpoint Cyber researchers Sam Decker and Nevan Beal said in an analysis published today. "Once deployed, it can profile the host,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Upcoming Speaking Engagements
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Frontier AI: The Genie's Out of the Bottle, but Where's the Rulebook?
item.description: Cutting-edge artificial intelligence models are deploying with more independence and less human oversight. Several state governments are trying to legislate transparency in their use.
item.dc:creator: Arielle Waldman

item.title: ClickFix's Mushrooming Ecosystem Demands New Defense Tactics
item.description: The attack vector is available for rent at scale, and evades AV and EDR, leaving YARA analysis as the best detection option.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: US: Pentagon Suspends CMMC Phase II Requirements for Defense Contractors
item.description: The US Department of Defense announced the immediate suspension of the CMMC Phase II requirements until further review

item.title: The AI Exchange: Innovators in Payment Security Featuring PCA Cyber Security
item.dc:creator: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: RabbitMQ Flaws Could Leak OAuth Secrets and Expose Cross-Tenant Queue Metadata
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of two access control-related flaws impacting the RabbitMQ message broker service that could allow attackers to leak OAuth client secrets, expose enterprise messaging infrastructure to takeover risks, and bypass tenant boundaries.
Miggo's security team, which discovered and reported the flaws, said one "leaks the broker's confidential OAuth
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: OpenAI Brings ChatGPT Work to Office Tasks: 3 Security Checks for IT Teams
item.description: <p>OpenAI’s ChatGPT Work can operate across connected apps, files, websites, and desktop software. IT teams should review permissions, approval rules, and audit visibility before expanding access.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-chatgpt-work-security-checks/">OpenAI Brings ChatGPT Work to Office Tasks: 3 Security Checks for IT Teams</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: Cursor IDE Auto-Executes Malicious Code in Poisoned Repos
item.description: Researchers reported the vulnerability to Cursor in December, but it still remains in the popular AI coding platform and can be exploited in poisoned repository attacks.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: 11 Old Microsoft-Signed Linux UEFI Shims Could Let Attackers Bypass Secure Boot
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have discovered 11 old, Microsoft-signed, Unified Extensible Firmware Interface (UEFI) applications that could be abused to bypass Secure Boot on most systems using the modern firmware standard.
"An attacker exploiting one of these vulnerable applications can execute untrusted code during system boot, enabling deployment of malicious UEFI bootkits or other malware,"
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: New MacOS Malware Exploits Legitimate Developer ID to Pose as Apple Crash Reporter
item.description: Researchers at Jamf Threat Labs detail CrashStealer, which steals passwords, cryptocurrency wallets and more

item.title: Study of 85 Crypto Wallet Extensions Finds Address Leaks and Cross-Site Tracking Risks
item.description:
Researchers at KU Leuven tested 85 of the most popular crypto wallets that run as browser extensions and found that the wallets themselves leak enough to link and track the people using them.
The way these wallets talk to websites and blockchain servers can tie a person's separate addresses together and let outsiders follow them from site to site. And on a site that already holds a name or
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: How Pentera Turns AI Security Workflows into Validation Engines
item.description:
AI security agents are starting to influence real security decisions. They summarize findings, prioritize remediation, recommend next steps, and help teams move faster. But most still rely on fragmented risk signals: scanner output, severity scores, threat intelligence, configuration findings, and exposure data.
That fragmentation matters because attackers do not move through environments one
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: OAuth Client ID Spoofing Lets Attackers Validate Stolen Microsoft Entra Credentials
item.description:
At least two distinct threat actors are weaponizing a novel evasion technique called OAuth client ID spoofing in cloud campaigns, while slipping past telemetry.
The activity allows users to enumerate user accounts and validate stolen credentials in Microsoft Entra ID environments, without ever generating a successful sign-in event that would otherwise alert defenders. And bad actors have begun
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Vulnerability in FIFA’s Network
item.description: <p>FIFA&#8217;s network was <a href="https://bobdahacker.com/blog/fifa-hack">vulnerable</a> to anyone with even minimal access.</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Lidl Notifies Customers of Third-Party Data Breach
item.description: Supermarket giant Lidl has revealed details of a supplier breach impacting customer data

item.title: Grok Build Uploaded Entire Git Repositories to xAI Storage, Not Just Files It Read
item.description:
xAI's Grok Build coding CLI was uploading entire Git repositories, full commit history and all, to a Google Cloud Storage bucket run by xAI, not just the files a coding task needed.
A researcher publishing as cereblab, testing version 0.2.93, captured one of those uploads, cloned the git bundle out of the intercepted request, and pulled back a file the agent had been told in plain terms not
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Forgotten UEFI shims undermining Secure Boot
item.description: ESET researchers discovered 11 vulnerable UEFI shim bootloaders signed by Microsoft that allow attackers to bypass UEFI Secure Boot by exploiting decade-old vulnerabilities

item.title: Five Charged in “Russian Coms” Fraud Platform Case
item.description: Five UK residents have been charged in relation to supplying Russian Coms fraud devices and apps

item.title: U.S. Sanctions First VPN Service and Malware Cryptor Seller Over Ransomware Support
item.description:
The U.S. Treasury Department's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) has designated two individuals and a VPN service provider for enabling ransomware actors' and other cybercriminals' malicious activities, including ransomware attacks against Americans.
The VPN, named First VPN Service (1VPNS), has been accused of offering its tools to ransomware groups, along with its 45-year-old Ukrainian
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: 148 npm Packages Disguised as Student Proxies Turned Browsers Into a DDoS Botnet
item.description:
A campaign of 148 npm packages disguised as student web proxies turned visitors' browsers into a distributed denial-of-service botnet for roughly two weeks in May, according to new research from&nbsp;JFrog.
The packages did not go after the developers who might install them. The operators used the registry as free hosting for a booby-trapped proxy site and let the students who came to dodge
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)
